### Cumulative Unique Users

The total number of distinct users who have signed in to CanadaLogin over a
chosen reporting window: this month, this calendar year, or since the
April 22, 2026 public launch.

This metric is useful for understanding the overall reach and adoption of
CanadaLogin over time. Note that it will not align with the sum of unique users
across individual apps: a user who signs into App A and App B on the same day
counts as two unique users at the app level but only one overall.

*Alternative names*:

- month-to-date unique users
- year-to-date unique users
- unique users since launch
- total overall unique users this month / this year
- overall unique users MTD / YTD
- distinct people reached on CanadaLogin

**Calculation.** Take the latest value of the cumulative unique-user column for
the window of interest. The window is chosen by *which column* you read, not by
a date filter (see [the note on reporting timespans](../notes.qmd#metrics-can-be-reported-over-different-timespans)):

- `mtd_unique_users` for month-to-date
- `ytd_unique_users` for year-to-date
- `since_apr22_unique_users` for since the launch

These columns are cumulative, so the most recent row holds the current total.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## SQL

Month-to-date:

```sql
SELECT
    mtd_unique_users
FROM ibm_verify.auth_total_logins
ORDER BY from_date DESC
LIMIT 1;
```

Year-to-date:

```sql
SELECT
    ytd_unique_users
FROM ibm_verify.auth_total_logins
ORDER BY from_date DESC
LIMIT 1;
```

Since launch:

```sql
SELECT
    since_apr22_unique_users
FROM ibm_verify.auth_total_logins
ORDER BY from_date DESC
LIMIT 1;
```

## R

Month-to-date:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("ibm_verify", "auth_total_logins")) |>
  slice_max(
    from_date,
    n = 1,
    with_ties = FALSE
  ) |>
  pull(mtd_unique_users)
```

Year-to-date:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("ibm_verify", "auth_total_logins")) |>
  slice_max(
    from_date,
    n = 1,
    with_ties = FALSE
  ) |>
  pull(ytd_unique_users)
```

Since launch:

```r
tbl(con, in_schema("ibm_verify", "auth_total_logins")) |>
  slice_max(
    from_date,
    n = 1,
    with_ties = FALSE
  ) |>
  pull(since_apr22_unique_users)
```

:::

*Interpretation notes.* Each column resets on its own cadence: `mtd_unique_users`
resets at the start of each month and `ytd_unique_users` at the start of each
calendar year. `since_apr22_unique_users` does not reset, so it grows
monotonically from launch. Do not sum these columns across rows; they are
already cumulative.
